title: Signs and Symbols
editorialSummary: Writing systems grouped by script type. False trail: Hiragana and Cherokee are both invented scripts (created by individual people) but Hiragana is a syllabary while Cherokee is a syllabary too — they correctly share the Syllabic Scripts group. Arabic is placed in Abjads (consonant-only scripts), not Alphabets, despite looking similar to an alphabet. 'Hebrew Script' is used rather than 'Hebrew' to avoid confusion with the language itself. Linear B is the Mycenaean Greek syllabary, deciphered in 1952 — verifiable in any classics reference.

meta.gptRationale: Script families are intellectually neat but require classification knowledge beyond everyday familiarity, especially for abjad versus syllabic distinctions.
